channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Juniper CEO: ‘I am disappointed and somewhat puzzled’ by DOJ merger rejection
item.description: Few would have predicted the U.S. Department of Justice would move to block HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. When it happened late last month, the two vendors and many industry watchers were caught off guard.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Zacks Investment hit in data breach - 12 million users potentially at risk
item.description: The company hasn't commented on the allegations yet, but users may wish to up their security protection.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Forget President’s Day sales, Apple is selling an M2 Mac mini refurb for just over $300 which could be the PC bargain of the year
item.description: A refurbished M2 Mac mini for $319 is a startling bargain, after Apple corrected prices on these devices to make sense compared to M4 models.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The scent of the mummy. Research discovers ancient Egyptian remains smell nice
item.description: Researchers who sniffed mummified remains in the name of science were greeted more with a blessing than a curse.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk will drop OpenAI offer if it remains a nonprofit: Lawyers
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk will withdraw its bid for OpenAI's nonprofit arm if the artificial intelligence firm halts its plans to become a for-profit entity, according to a new court filing. "If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to delete multiple text messages on a Mac and iPad
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains the steps to declutter text messages on a Mac or iPad. Apple offers ways to do this more efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 reasons Microsoft needn’t fear DeepSeek
item.description: The release of the latest version of the Chinese genAI bot DeepSeek last month upended the tech world when its creators claimed it was built for only $6 million — far less than the hundreds of billions of dollars Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Meta, and others have poured into genAI development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco data center switches feature baked-in security for AI, networking duties
item.description: Cisco has unwrapped a new family of data center switches it says will help customers more securely support large workloads and facilitate AI development across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why the generative AI hype is good
item.description: Elon Musk predicts artificial general intelligence (AGI) by 2025. Former OpenAI employee Leopold Aschenbrenner says AGI by 2027. Futurist Ray Kurzweil bets on 2029 for AI surpassing human intelligence. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever declared that language models are “slightly conscious.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is your camera ticket a fake? Here’s how to tell
item.description: Red light camera tickets: Not liable to ID the driver; some are fishing expeditions. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you fight back against tricky fake tickets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
